threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still look shocked when someone casually brings up the latest Tube strike over a pint at the Queen's. That's a sure sign you're new.
Last night on the 333, a guy tried to sell mint-condition vinyl records. Totally out of place, but everyone was vibing with him. Brixton at its quirky best.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I heard a guy juggling flaming torches outside the Spätkauf while a group of kids danced to techno—pure Neukölln magic.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin can you board a packed U-Bahn and hear a guy playing Beethoven on a theremin. It's a weird mix of classical and chaos that feels uniquely ours.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s a local when they know to avoid the Civic Center BART station after a loud bang—always a sign of something going wrong.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a Muni bus today that stopped for a street musician, and the whole crowd broke into spontaneous applause. Only in SF.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think the park trees are for shade, but locals know they're just hiding the best spots for vermut.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by their insistence on taking the metro for everything, especially when the stroll through Gràcia’s winding streets is half the charm.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still wait for a polite smile on the bus—hard to find in Dalston. Instead, it's all about that intense stare out the window.
Last night, caught a guy on the 35 bus serenading a fellow passenger with an off-key rendition of 'Wonderwall'. The whole bus was in stitches.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: Walking past the Turkish market, I realized I could name every spice as the old lady waved at me; that’s when I felt like it was home.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Kreuzberg's street art changes weekly, but the 'Kreuzberg monkey' graffiti remains untouched. It’s like a secret keeper of the neighborhood's vibe.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell a local by how they navigate BART delays like it’s just another Tuesday. Outsiders are still standing there confused.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a Muni bus stop mid-route because a guy was practicing his sax solo on the sidewalk, crowd cheered. Classic SF vibe.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists always miss the quiet, tiny plazas in Gràcia. They crowd the main square, but the real charm is in the hidden corners with local cafes.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They’re the ones staring at the metro map like it's an ancient treasure map, trying to decipher the lines while missing the train.
